name: Buttermilk Bread in a Pressure Cooker
recipeIngredient: 1 Cup Buttermilk (I don't have the recipe at hand, but you can make quite adequate buttermilk from dried milk)
4 Cups Water in pressure cooker with rack.
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine egg and sugar.
2. Sift flour, soda, and baking powder.
3. Add buttermilk and alternately with the sifted dry ingredients. Mix well.
4. Add nuts and stuff into a buttered bowl. Cover with wax paper.
5. Place the bowl in the cooker on a rack with water.
6. Place the cover on the cooker.
7. Steam without pressure valve for 15 minutes, then cook at high pressure for 25 minutes.
8. Allow to cool naturally.
Works and makes quite tasty bread. I've also got a really good recipe for making brown bread and Boston brown bread in a pressure cooker. After a week at sea, we (a crew of four) could polish off a whole loaf at one sitting, especially if the butter was still firm.
